mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through increased household income and reduced unemployment risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139,004k as of 2014-07-01 indicates sustained hiring
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce higher average 20-day returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint signals monetary accommodation that suppresses real discount rates and inflates equity valuations. This regime produces systematically positive 20-day forward returns as investors reach for yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2014-07-01 confirms zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not significantly higher than periods when FEDFUNDS >= 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals synchronized demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors. This interaction effect amplifies confidence in sustained economic growth, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The mechanism fails if either component weakens without offsetting strength in the other.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 893k and RSAFS at 439,802M as of 2014-06-01 and 2014-07-01
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS momentum) falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of joint strength do not produce higher 20-day returns than periods of single-sector strength (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2% annualized signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum. This mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns as rising production indicates strong order books and pricing power. The signal activates only when INDPRO growth exceeds the post-crisis trend.
regime_hint: manufacturing expansion with accelerating capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of above-trend INDPRO growth do not produce significantly higher 60-day returns than below-trend periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger Federal Reserve easing expectations. This mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward before policy accommodation materializes.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration triggering policy response expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE increases and 20-day forward returns is positive (> 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown that compresses equity risk premiums. This mechanism predicts negative 5-day forward returns as investors front-run recession concerns. The signal is most powerful in late-expansion regimes when growth expectations are elevated.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns is positive (> 0.01) over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of sub-2% growth produce negative 5-day returns in < 52% of cases (binomial test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) with declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates virtuous labor market cycle that amplifies consumer confidence and discretionary spending. This interaction effect produces positive 60-day forward returns as the dual confirmation strengthens the growth narrative.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139,004k and UNRATE at 6.2% as of 2014-07-01
regime_hint: synchronized labor market improvement with falling unemployment and rising employment
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE decline) falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of joint improvement do not produce higher 60-day returns than periods of single-metric improvement (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, a negative real rate regime emerges that erodes bond yields and forces equity valuations higher. This mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns as investors flee fixed income for equities seeking real returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.69 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2014-06-01 and 2014-07-01
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative monetary policy and rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (inflation × negative real rate) falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in < 58% of cases (binomial test p > 0.15).
